description_long: This is a tall, multi-tiered pagoda structure with seven distinct stories, each marked by wide, overhanging roofs that curve upward at the edges. The roofs are a striking teal color, contrasting with the dark brown and gray of the main building body. The base is solid and rectangular, with a stone-like texture and some green patches suggesting landscaping or a foundation. The uppermost level is capped with a slender spire or finial, adding to the vertical emphasis of the design. The pagoda features ornamental details along the roof ridges and eaves, typical of East Asian architecture. The overall form is symmetrical, with each story slightly smaller than the one below, creating a tiered, elegant silhouette.
description: A seven-story teal-roofed pagoda with upward-curving eaves, ornamental finial, and a solid, stone-like base. The structure is richly detailed and symmetrical, embodying traditional East Asian architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance area, with the base partially open and the tiered roofs prominently stacked above.
description_view_1: Side view with the full width of the pagoda visible, highlighting the symmetry and the layered teal roofs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pagoda, with the same tiered roof structure and a solid, uninterrupted base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, but with minor differences in the landscaping and base details.
